item.title: INTERVIEW: SBS speaks to Travis Lovett on his 800km Walk for Truth
item.description: Kerrupmara Gunditjmara man Travis Lovett is now well into his 800 kilometre walk from Melbourne to Canberra. The former Yoorrook Justice Commissioner is leading the five-week long 'Walk for Truth' to call on Prime Minister Anthony Albanese to establish a National Truth Telling Process. SBS reporter Angelica Waite spoke with Mr Lovett when he was in Seymour, about 150 kilometres into his journey.

item.title: A $1.8m wine loss — and the growing crisis facing Australia's small producers
item.description: As natural disasters intensify, small producers are left navigating loss, limited support and an uncertain furture.

item.title: Trump rejects Iran's latest proposal to end war
item.description: The US has said it did not want to exit ⁠its confrontation with ‌Iran early "and then have ‌the problem arise in three more years".

item.title: Six Australian Gaza aid flotilla activists released in Greece
item.description: Three released Australian activists have said they were subjected to violence and mistreated while on board an Israeli vessel.

item.title: Government set to pull back budget spending as delayed economic shock threatens jobs
item.description: Economists have warned that Australia's historically strong employment rate could be at risk from global pressures.

item.title: Australia's student visa crackdown hits record highs — what it means for who gets in
item.description: Australia's student visa crackdown is reshaping who gets in — and exposing a system driven as much by politics as policy.

item.title: Colombians divided over the fate of hippos linked to Pablo Escobar
item.description: Colombia is confronting a unique problem: what to do with a population of hippopotamuses originally linked to Pablo Escobar. The notorious late drug lord became infamous for importing the creatures and establishing a zoo at one of his properties.

item.title: INTERVIEW: North Aboriginal Justice Agency CEO accuses NT government of long term detention in watchhouses
item.description: The Northern Territory justice system is under fresh scrutiny amid allegations people are being held in overcrowded cells for long periods of time without adequate healthcare in a Darwin correctional facility. A spokesperson for the Department of Corrections has said in a statement that the Darwin Transitional Custody Centre accommodates prisoners for a maximum of four weeks. They said prisoners undergo intake and medical screening to ensure they don't have any serious health concerns and are well suited to the environment. The spokesperson also said prisoners do have access to phones and can contact family. But this week, the North Australia Aboriginal Justice Agency (NAAJA) accused the government of failing to fully address the issue, saying parts of the Darwin City Watchhouse had been renamed and transferred to the control of the corrections department – while conditions continue to resemble a watchhouse. NAAJA CEO Ben Grimes is being interviewed here by Tee Mitchell.

item.title: Trump to review Iran's new proposal, warns of renewed US strike
item.description: The US president has said he "can't imagine" that Iran's latest proposal would be acceptable to him.

item.title: 'Hard to swallow': Australia's Italian citizenship hopefuls dealt a blow by court
item.description: The ruling reinforces controversial laws enacted last year that limit eligibility through distant ancestry.

item.title: It's probably already in your fridge — and it could help in cutting risk of Alzheimer's
item.description: Eating seafood twice a week can be important to protect the brain from different diseases.

item.title: 'A worrying pattern': Man arrested after attack on nun in Israel
item.description: Christians in Israel experienced over 150 incidents of harassment and violence in 2025, according to one inter-religious organisation.

item.title: Leagh accidentally saw a photo on her partner's phone. It put her in an impossible position
item.description: These Australians found themselves caught in moral dilemmas. Here's what they decided.

item.title: The mental health 'safety gap' causing decade-long delays for vulnerable Australians
item.description: From referral to consultation, accessing mental health care presents many challenges for some Australians, a new report has found.

item.title: A tanker hit by projectiles in the Strait of Hormuz | SBS News in Easy English 4 May 2026
item.description: A daily 5-minute news bulletin for English learners and people with a disability.

item.title: Savings over spending: Chalmers says upcoming budget his 'most responsible yet'
item.description: Federal Treasurer Jim Chalmers has promised to deliver his most responsible budget yet with savings expected across every portfolio. Defending possible changes to negative gearing and capital gains taxes, the government says this budget is about adapting to the uncertain circumstances facing Australia's economy.

item.title: RBA expected to lift rates as Australian dollar nears four year high
item.description: Australian shares dropped 0.4 per cent ahead of an expected Reserve Bank rate rise, with losses across most sectors led by consumer staples and energy, while petrol prices continued to ease nationwide. Corporate headlines included a recall by A2 Milk in the United States, an ASIC investigation into Accent Group, and NAB’s weaker half-year profit driven by accounting changes despite solid lending growth and a steady dividend. The outlook for borrowers and interest rates is in focus as the Australian dollar lifts above 72 US cents. George Boubouras from K2 Asset Management Ltd joins the podcast to break down the market moves and what to watch next.

item.title: Australian Gaza aid flotilla activist returns home following Israeli detention
item.description: Two other non-Australian flotilla participants continue to be detained by Israeli authorities.

item.title: The RBA's rates call could 'come down to the wire'. This is what it means for you
item.description: If the RBA decides to increase the cash rate, it will be the third hike this year.

item.title: Ukraine and Russia declare separate ceasefires as enmity deepens
item.description: Political quarrelling has deepened between the two countries as the US' diplomatic eye has been drawn to the Middle East.

item.title: Three dead in a boating tragedy off the far north coast of New South Wales | SBS News in Easy English 5 May
FIFA World Cup preparations ramp up for the Socceroos
item.description: The Socceroos are ramping up their World Cup preparations, with just over a month until the 2026 tournament begins in North America. Coach Tony Popovic has a lot to do... and everyone has a lot to look forward to.

item.title: Does the Royal Commission into Antisemitism and Social Cohesion go far enough?
item.description: The first two days of public hearings for the Royal Commission into Antisemitism and Social Cohesion has heard from 24 witnesses who are Jewish Australians with lived experience of antisemitism. While there is broad community support for a Royal Commission, some argue it should be expanded to include other minority perspectives including Muslim and First Nations Australians' experiences of racism.

item.title: Seven cases of hantavirus now identified on cruise ship stranded off west Africa
item.description: Seven cases of the deadly hantavirus have now been identified in a suspected outbreak on a luxury cruise ship off West Africa. The outbreak has caused global concern, as the disease is considered rare among humans. What is happening with the ship, and what does hantavirus actually entail?

item.title: Experts warn Australians are underestimating flu risk as winter looms
item.description: More than 1,700 deaths were recorded last flu season, but experts say Australians still underestimate the risks.

item.title: Antisemitism hearing told man's public assault largely ignored on busy street
item.description: Nir Golan now covers his kippah with a baseball cap.

item.title: Interest rates rise again as markets slip and mortgage pressure builds
item.description: The Reserve Bank has lifted interest rates for the third time this year, pushing the cash rate to 4.35 per cent, even as other major economies pause. In this episode, PWC Chief Economist Amy Lomas unpacks what’s driving the decision and what it signals for the months ahead. The ASX edged lower, falling 0.2 per cent, with Todd Hoare from LGT Wealth Management breaking down how investors reacted. And with borrowers facing rising costs, Sally Tindall from Canstar explains what the latest rate hike means for mortgage repayments and how to assess whether your loan is still competitive.

item.title: Sina was forced to weigh a 'dreaded' decision — and fears the rate hike will spark another
item.description: The Reserve Bank of Australia has warned that the economic outlook is not good for Australians.

item.title: Trump pauses day-old Hormuz operation in latest push for Iran deal
item.description: The so-called "Project Freedom" was supposed to help vessels depart the Strait of Hormuz under US guard.

item.title: Trump pauses day-old Hormuz operation in latest push for Iran deal
item.description: The so-called "Project Freedom" was supposed to help vessels depart the Strait of Hormuz under US guard.

item.title: Teen radicalisation fears drive major online counter‑terror overhaul
item.description: Spies will monitor gaming platforms as authorities warn extremists are targeting young people through gaming platforms and chat groups.

item.title: AFL to introduce new mental health rules after record fine to Carlton
item.description: Carlton have been fined $75,000 over their handling of an incident involving player Elijah Hollands in a game against Collingwood in April. The fine will be paid to the AFL's mental health partner, Headspace. The finding has highlighted the treatment of mental health within sporting circles.

item.title: Hantavirus may have spread between people on a cruise ship. What are the risks?
item.description: Human-to-human transmission of hantavirus has been confirmed on a cruise ship. It sounds alarming — but experts say it's very different from Covid.

item.title: A group of women and children linked to IS Group returning to Australia | SBS News in Easy English 6 May 2026
Bondi accused charged with 19 new offences while in custody
item.description: The new charges include ten counts of shooting with intent to murder during Australia's deadliest mass shooting in almost three decades.

item.title: Roblox recruitment: Why so many kids are falling into extremism
item.description: Online gaming platforms like Roblox and Minecraft have become increasingly popular tools for extremist groups to recruit young people. With Australia's terror threat level at 'probable', the government has announced a new Counter Terrorism Online Centre to disrupt and divert terrorist networks from radicalising young Australians.

item.title: Eta Aquarids will light up Australian skies. Here's how to see the meteor shower
item.description: The meteor shower will decorate Australian skies, but experts warn light pollution could hinder city views.

item.title: 'A different world': What the Reserve Bank's three future scenarios mean for Australia
item.description: There are three possible outlooks for the Australian economy that the RBA is considering — one "difficult" and two worse.

item.title: Rising living costs hitting some older Australians hardest and the Australian dollar at fresh 4 year high
item.description: Senior Australians on government payments are facing some of the sharpest cost of living increases in the country as soaring electricity prices and rising interest rates put pressure on household budgets. In this episode of the SBS On the Money podcast, Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Patricia Sparrow from COTA Australia to find out more, before Laura Cooper from Nuveen shares her outlook on interest rates after the Reserve Bank’s latest decision. Plus, Luke Laretive from Seneca Financial Solutions breaks down a strong rally on the Australian sharemarket as the ASX200 surged following another record session on Wall Street.

item.title: 'Non-event': World Cup hotel bookings lagging far behind expectations, survey shows
item.description: US President Donald Trump has frequently touted the country's host status for world football's centrepiece event, but he has also launched a wide-ranging crackdown on visas for visitors to the United States.

item.title: Fiji, Australia ink treaty over climate change
'You're building something tangible': The offline trend pulling people off screens
item.description: A new generation is picking up the threads, turning knitting into a statement on sustainability and self-expression.

item.title: Russia-Ukraine ceasefire crumbles within hours after drone, missile attacks reported
item.description: Ukraine reported Russian drone attacks just hours after a unilaterally announced ceasefire was supposed to begin.

item.title: Socceroos matches will return to Federation Square after state government intervenes
item.description: Victorian Premier Jacinta Allan has overruled a controversial decision to cancel live screenings ahead of the World Cup in June.

item.title: WHO says hantavirus is not the new COVID, as passengers evacuated from outbreak cruise ship
item.description: The cruise ship linked to a deadly hantavirus outbreak has left Cape Verde and is headed for the Canary Islands, where passengers are expected to depart the vessel and begin heading home. Three people have died in the outbreak with eight recorded cases so far. But the World Health Organisation is moving to reassure people hantavirus is not the new COVID-19.

item.title: Clash between One Nation volunteer and Liberal senator | SBS News in Easy English 7 May 2026
item.description: A daily five minute news wrap for English learners and people with disability.

item.title: 'Will not please everyone': Government details reservation scheme for gas exporters
item.description: While some stakeholders have described it as "a winner", others have criticised the proposed policy as a distraction.

item.title: Healing through art : Stories of First Nations people who have experienced incarceration
item.description: First Nations Australians who have lived experience of incarceration in Victoria are showcasing their work through an art exhibition. The exhibition, highlighting the artists' voices, has proved the ripple effect - where one event can cause a series of bigger effects - for some to take a new path beyond prison.

item.title: Is Trump’s ‘Epic Fury’ over? IS group 'brides' return home & a flatpack forehead slap?!
item.description: The latest pause in the US-Iran conflict has the world holding its breath - has the White House lost control of its own strategy? National security concerns surround the return of IS-linked women and children to Australia and a major election victory for Narendra Modi sparks claims of election fraud in India. Plus, the surprisingly brutal world of competitive furniture building.

item.title: Snow in May? What Australia's cold snap means for this winter
item.description: Climate experts have shared predictions for this winter, as snow falls in parts of Australia.

item.title: ASX soars, Aussie dollar at 4-year high on hopes of war’s end
item.description: The Australian sharemarket rose 0.96 per cent, amid hopes of a peace deal between the US and Iran. As a result, oil prices have dropped sharply – though remain above US$100 a barrel – and precious metal and commodity prices have risen. The Australian dollar has also benefited, reaching a four-year high, as risk appetite increases. For more on this, Stephanie Youssef spoke with Angus Geddes, Chief Investment Officer at Fat Prophets.

item.title: Australia's growing 'silent killer' could claim thousands of lives by century's end
item.description: Some 2,288 Australian communities were analysed to project how heatwaves could reshape death rates by 2100. Here what was found.

item.title: An uneasy relationship with diversity emerges in Farrer ahead of One Nation vote test
item.description: Eighteen months after white supremacists marched down Farrer's streets, the electorate's views on migration are being tested.

item.title: WHO warns of more hantavirus cases in 'limited' outbreak
item.description: As contact tracing efforts continue, the World Health Organization again stressed that it expects the outbreak to be limited.

item.title: 'Second Chance': Focus on children as IS group-linked families return to Australia
item.description: A group of Australian women and children with links to the self-proclaimed IS group have returned to Australia after spending years in a Syrian detention camp. Three of the women have been arrested upon their arrival. Now, authorities say they are focused on the ensuring the wellbeing and reintegration of children among the group.

item.title: Federal budget to produce $64 billion in savings | SBS News in Easy English 8 May 2026
item.description: A news bulletin for those learning English and those with disability

item.title: Five key takeaways from the first week of antisemitism royal commission hearings
item.description: As the antisemitism royal commission wraps up its first week of hearings, here is what the inquiry has heard so far.

item.title: Australia's 'most liveable city' is also one of its most affordable — but will it last?
item.description: This capital city's housing market has stumbled in recent years, but supply and population trends may be setting up a turn.

item.title: An inflation headache, a stronger Aussie dollar, and falling fuel prices — for now
item.description: The key money stories this week, broken down simply and what they mean for you.

item.title: A $100 billion Australian sector is struggling. For Angela, it means ‘working twice as hard’
item.description: Soaring costs are hurting Australia’s 2.7 million small businesses – especially in hospitality. So, what can the federal government’s May budget offer?

item.title: INTERVIEW: Professor Jessica Rosenberg on encouraging young people to engage on quantum technologies
item.description: How should Australia be preparing students for new technologies? It’s an issue governments and educators are grappling with across the world. American expert in the field, Professor Jessica Rosenberg from George Mason University, has been developing a school curriculum to encourage more young people to engage on quantum technologies. She’s also keen to see more girls and women in the field. During her visit to Australia she sat down with Chief Political Correspondent Anna Henderson to discuss the quantum task ahead.

item.title: Russia and Ukraine agree to renewed three-day ceasefire and prisoner swap, Trump says
item.description: The US president said the halt in fighting for Victory Day celebrations could mark the "beginning of the end" of the war.

item.title: A $100 billion Australian sector is struggling. For Angela, it means 'working twice as hard'
item.description: As costs soar for small businesses, tax relief and reduced red tape are tipped as support delivered in the federal budget.

item.title: Iran accuses US of 'reckless military adventure' after empty oil tankers struck
item.description: The US military claims it struck the ships in the Strait of Hormuz after they tried to breach its blockade on Iranian ports.

item.title: How do Australians really feel about each other? New data paints an 'alarming' picture
item.description: Widespread declines were observed in a survey exploring how much Australians trust each other and the country's institutions.

item.title: One Nation claims historic win in Farrer in poll seen as litmus test for Coalition
item.description: Voters in the southern New South Wales seat of Farrer have headed to the polls to choose a new MP after their former representative Sussan Ley resigned from parliament. Analysts say the vote could have lasting consequences for the coalition.

item.title: Eleven million people, just 500 doctors. How Australia's neighbour manages healthcare
item.description: It's Australia's closest neighbour and biggest aid recipient. But PNG's healthcare crisis has major impacts on patients and staff.

item.title: Maria has 13 children. It has its challenges, but there's one thing she's sure of
item.description: Maria El Khoury's children describe her as a strong role model who is patient, selfless and caring.

item.title: INTERVIEW: Health Minister Mark Butler ahead of the Budget
item.description: There are big changes ahead for the NDIS with some budget cuts and tightened eligibility. Health Minister Mark Butler says the government aims to remove around 160,000 participants by the end of the decade. Children with autism are the largest cohort of NDIS participants, with many set to be moved into the government's new thriving kids program and he says future eligibility will be based on a different set of criteria. He says work is also being done on cultural safety before Indigenous participants are transitioned off the NDIS under the new reform measures. Mr Butler's been talking the SBS Chief Political Correspondent Anna Henderson.

item.title: 'Unfair, unacceptable': Jim Chalmers flags major tax changes in upcoming budget
item.description: The treasurer also said Australians are paying a "hefty price" for the war in the Middle East, but pushed back on recession fears.

item.title: 'Not another COVID': WHO urges calm ahead of evacuation of hantavirus-hit cruise ship
item.description: Authorities in the Canary Islands are preparing to evacuate a cruise ship after a hantavirus outbreak killed three passengers.

item.title: INTERVIEW: SBS News speaks to Treasurer Jim Chalmers ahead of the federal budget
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers talks One Nation, housing affordability and the economy with SBS World News Chief Political Correspondent Anna Henderson

item.title: Could One Nation's Farrer victory be the 'beginning of the end' for the Coalition?
item.description: The Farrer by-election delivered a historic result that could represent an "existential situation" for the Coalition.

item.title: One in two Australians is experiencing workplace burnout. Nicole found a solution
item.description: What are the factors keeping many people in the rat race — and what are the ones driving others out?

item.title: Australia's dying middle class — and the rise of an inherited wealth nation
item.description: The wealth of Australia's middle-class has flatlined over five years but there's one group whose assets have soared.

item.title: Trump rejects Iran's peace proposal response as more drone attacks hit Gulf region
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu insisted there is still nuclear material to be removed from Iran.

item.title: Trump rejects Iran's peace proposal response as more drone attacks hit Gulf region
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu insisted there is still nuclear material to be removed from Iran.

item.title: Australia's isolation plan 'one of the stronger responses' to hantavirus globally, Butler says
item.description: Australians evacuated from the MV Hondius will quarantine for weeks in a pandemic-era facility outside Perth.

item.title: Albanese vowed no changes to housing tax breaks. Now he's defending reforms
item.description: The federal government is making the case for controversial reforms to housing tax policies.

item.title: Passengers on board hantavirus-hit ship return home
item.description: The World Health Organisation says there is low risk to the public, after those on board the hantavirus-hit cruise ship disembarked in Tenerife. Passengers and crew aboard the ship are being repatriated to their home countries, with the W-H-O urging regular health monitoring and 42-day quarantine.

item.title: SBS News in Easy English 11 May 2026
item.description: A daily five minute news wrap for English learners and people with disability.

item.title: SBS News in Easy English 11 May 2026
item.description: A daily five minute news wrap for English learners and people with disability.

item.title: Trump says Iranian response totally unacceptable
item.description: United States President, Donald Trump, has called Iran's response to a proposal to end the war totally unacceptable. The US proposal sought to end fighting before starting talks on more contentious issues, including Iran's nuclear program. Iranian state TV says that country's response focused on ending the war on all fronts, including Lebanon, and on the safety of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, without indicating how or when the vital waterway might re-open.

item.title: Why CSL sank, and what the Federal Budget could mean for investors
item.description: The ASX 200 slipped 0.5 per cent ahead of the Federal Budget as oil prices surged and investors weighed the prospect of tax reform. Banks were weaker across the board, while CSL plunged 16 per cent after cutting earnings expectations and flagging a major impairment.

item.title: What time is the 2026 federal budget? Here's everything we know so far
item.description: Here are some of the big-ticket items that will impact Australians.

item.title: Scrolling through his phone after his marriage ended, Tim realised he had no one to call
item.description: For some men, a lack of emotional resources means profound struggle and suicidal ideation can follow breakups.

item.title: Everything you need to know about Eurovision 2026
item.description: Here's how to watch and what to know about the 70th Eurovision Song Contest.

item.title: Angry Trump rejection puts US-Iran truce on 'life support'
item.description: As the US again attempts to pile pressure on its regime, Iran says it is ready 'for any eventuality'.

item.title: The 48-hour deadline DFAT faces for Australians returning from hantavirus-hit cruise
item.description: Four Australians, a New Zealand citizen and one permanent resident will spend three weeks in a quarantine facility near Perth.

item.title: 'Brutal oppression': Australian government imposes new sanctions on Iranian officials
item.description: The Australian government has imposed over 230 sanctions on Iranian individuals and entities to date.

item.title: SBS News in Easy English 12 May 2026
item.description: A daily 5 minute news wrap for English learners and people with disability.

item.title: Town 'at the end of the world' denies hantavirus origins as travellers warned
item.description: Authorities have pointed the finger at an Argentine tourism hotspot, but locals have accused the government of a "smear campaign".

item.title: US and Iran still at an impasse
item.description: Discussions on a peace deal between the United States and Iran appear to be at an impasse, and US President Donald Trump described the ceasefire between the two sides as unbelievably weak. He described Iran's response to the latest proposal from the US as a piece of garbage that he didn't finish reading. Iran says it has not made excessive demands and says the US is the one trying to impose unreasonable demands.

item.title: BHP reclaims crown as Australia's largest listed company
item.description: BHP surged to a record high and overtook Commonwealth Bank as Australia’s largest listed company, helping lift the materials sector evenas the ASX200 fell for a third straight session. In this episode of SBS On the Money, Jonathan Shead from State Street Investment Management unpack BHP’s milestone, the strength in mining stocks and the broader investment environment. NAB Head of Australian Economics Gareth Spence also joins, to explain why business conditions have dropped to their weakest level since 2020 and how the conflict in the Middle East is driving up costs across supply chains.

item.title: Who are the winners and losers of the 2026 federal budget?
item.description: Small business owners, first home buyers, migrants and travellers are just some of the Australians impacted by the 2026-27 federal budget.

item.title: Your five-minute guide to the budget: Key changes Australians should know
item.description: The key measures, major announcements and biggest takeaways from the federal budget — explained simply.

item.title: 2026-27 federal budget migration numbers: What's changing and who's affected
item.description: The budget has confirmed the number of permanent migrants to be allowed into Australia.

item.title: Treasurer cites 'hard road of reform' as he reveals 2026 federal budget
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers has delivered his 2026 budget, which he says takes what he's called the 'hard road of reform'. At the heart of the budget are housing tax changes, and a $35 billion saving from reform to the runaway National Disability Insurance Scheme. Labor has been keen to portray this budget as one of spending restraint, after warnings by economists and Reserve Bank governor Michele Bullock that splashing out would only fuel inflation and risk further rate hikes.

item.title: Treasurer flags 'fairer' budget as business groups criticise tax reforms
item.description: The Federal Budget has been handed down, including major tax changes that have been welcomed by unions, social services groups, and renters groups. Industry groups have raised concerns, saying the tax changes will be bad for business, as debate continues over how to balance fairness and fiscal restraint.

item.title: A drawing my son did at kindergarten made me quit my corporate job
item.description: Terry Murton says his obsession with climbing the corporate ladder stopped him from being the father and husband he wanted to be.

item.title: Why the budget might not 'shift the dial' on home ownership
item.description: The government says its housing tax reforms will help first home buyers — but some are doubtful.

item.title: 'Devoid of all military logic': Zelenskyy fumes as Russia targets Ukraine civilians
item.description: The attacks came following the end of a three-day ceasefire to mark the Soviet victory in World War Two.

item.title: Investors, renters react cautiously to Australia changing negative gearing, capital gains tax
item.description: Speculation was rife in the lead up to this year's budget about what it would contain for housing investors. Now we know what Treasurer Jim Chalmers has planned for the capital gains tax discount, and negative gearing regime, both of which have been blamed for favouring investors over first home buyers and inflating prices. What do the changes mean for renters and potential buyers in the housing market?

item.title: President Donald Trump set to arrive in Beijing SBS | News in Easy English 13 May 2026
item.description: A five minute bulletin for English language learners

item.title: 'Slinging margaritas': FBI head Kash Patel in heated senate exchange over drinking claims
item.description: Kash Patel has filed a $345 million lawsuit over a news article that painted an unflattering picture of his leadership.

item.title: Five things buried in the federal budget you didn't know about
item.description: Hidden among the major announcements are some of the budget's more curious spending measures.

item.title: British Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer defies more internal calls for his resignation
item.description: British Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer insists he has no intention of resigning as calls grow louder within his own Labour Party for him to step down after historic losses last week in local council elections. Starmer's insistence to stay has seen some of the junior members of his government quit in protest.

item.title: Critics pay out on PM for 'cowardly' gambling reforms
item.description: The government has finally filed its formal response to the gambling inquiry chaired by the late MP Peta Murphy. But their response has drawn sharp criticism from advocates, who say it was dropped the day of the federal Budget - and that it doesn't go far enough.

item.title: We asked what you wanted to know about the budget. Here are the answers
item.description: SBS News asked audiences what mattered most to them in the federal budget. These were the key questions.

item.title: 'Devastated' disability community reacts to federal budget
item.description: Cuts to the National Disability Insurance Scheme are the largest source of savings in the federal budget, leaving many in the disability community angry and anxious about what's to come. The details of which participants will lose their supports, and whether state governments will fill those gaps, are still being worked out.

item.title: First Eurovision semi in the books
item.description: The heart of the 2026 Eurovision song contest has begun in Vienna, with the first semi-final now in the books, and all eyes turning to the second semi-final, in which Australian Delta Goodrem will compete.

item.title: 'Grandfathering' vs new builds: The key housing terms and who will get a tax perk
item.description: While the changes privilege "people on the basis of birth order", they have been largely welcomed by economists.

item.title: On The Money: What Budget 2026 means for property, investments and the economy
item.description: The ASX closed lower, after a record 10.4 per cent slump in CommBank shares wiped around $25 billion from its market value, while investors digested major Federal Budget reforms targeting negative gearing and capital gains tax.

item.title: Budget 2026: SBS goes one-on-one with Prime Minister Anthony Albanese
item.description: SBS chief political correspondent Anna Henderson quizzes the Prime Minister about the budget, broken promises, a gas tax, and more

item.title: Indigenous Australia reacts to the 2026 federal budget
item.description: The Federal Government says it's investing $1.2 billion in First Nations communities and Closing the Gap over five years as part of Tuesday's budget. Headline measures include new funding for remote jobs, community controlled health infrastructure, and programs to combat gendered violence. Despite these investments, some community leaders have told NITV they feel the budget left First Nations people behind.

item.title: Australia v the world: How our spending compares globally
item.description: Budgets inevitably involve trade-offs, so how do Australia's priorities line up with the world?

item.title: 'No one seems to know': Albanese dismisses gas export tax hike calls as 'slogans'
item.description: Anthony Albanese says the government is prioritising the supply of fuel.

item.title: INTERVIEW: Delta Goodrem on what it means to represent Australia in the Eurovision Song Contest
item.description: Award-winning singer-songwriter Delta Goodrem is representing Australia in the Eurovision Song Contest, with her song Eclipse. Eleven years on since Australia officially joined the globally-adored song contest, there are high hopes for Delta's performance. Attracting an audience of 2.2 million Australian viewers last year, all eyes will be on the star as she takes the stage in Vienna on Friday morning Australian time. SBS' Chief International Correspondent Ben Lewis sat down with Delta to chat all things Eurovision and what it means to represent her country.

item.title: The 13 'Down Down' tickets behind Coles' court loss — and what the ruling could mean
item.description: Justice Michael O'Bryan found Coles had misled consumers in a judgment handed down on Thursday morning.

item.title: 'I will not apologise': Independent senator Tammy Tyrrell joins the Labor Party
item.description: The Tasmanian senator has detailed the reasons behind her second flip in as many years.

item.title: An empty $400 million facility and 'upskilling' staff: Inside Australia's hantavirus test
item.description: Australia's response to the outbreak, which began on a cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean, stands in contrast to other countries.

item.title: Judge rules Coles misled customers in 'Down Down' campaign | Midday News Bulletin 14 May 2026
item.description: A judge rules Coles misled customers in its 'Down Down' pricing campaign; Independent Tasmanian Senator Tammy Tyrell defects to Labor; and in football, Iran's football team is sent off by supporters, ahead of a controversial World Cup campaign.

item.title: Despite the war, this US city is welcoming Iran’s national team with 'open arms'
item.description: Preparations are underway in Tucson, Arizona, to host Iran's World Cup squad despite uncertainty over the conflict.

item.title: Coles misled customers in pricing campaign | SBS News in Easy English 14 May 2026
item.description: A five minute bulletin for English language learners

item.title: 'Stronger approach': Update in mission to return Australians who were on hantavirus-hit ship
item.description: An aircraft and crew will arrive in the Netherlands on Thursday to repatriate six people to Australia.

item.title: Football chief seeks Iraqi citizenship for Australian coach Graham Arnold
item.description: Former Australian national coach Graham Arnold has become a hero in the Middle Eastern country.

item.title: Trade and Iran dominate US-China talks in Beijing
item.description: US President Donald Trump has met with Chinese president Xi Jinping in Beijing, after receiving a warm welcome at the Great Wall of China. Trade tariffs and the war in the Middle East were high on the agenda.

item.title: ACCC says Coles ruling a warning to all retailers | Evening News Bulletin 14 May 2026
item.description: The competition regulator says Federal Court's ruling on Coles is a warning to all retailers, Australians on hantavirus cruise to return tomorrow, New Zealand prepares for the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Trump and Xi face-off, Starmer staggers on, while Macron has a word…
item.description: Taiwan on tenterhooks as they watch Donald Trump in Beijing for a high-stakes summit with Xi Jinping. The Iran war enters its eleventh week with ceasefire talks faltering and leadership challenges plague the UK Prime Minister. Plus, Eurovision faces boycotts over Israel’s participation and France’s President, Emmanuel Macron, chides a Nairobi crowd.

item.title: Four audience members ejected from Eurovision over 'disruptive behaviour' during Israel set
item.description: Israel's contestant at the Eurovision Song Contest semi-final says he heard the boos of a small number of protesters as he ‌took the stage.

item.title: The Aussies who could be caught in the middle of the housing tax shake-up
item.description: Tax changes designed to help younger Australians into the housing market could also create unintended consequences, experts say.

item.title: Xero CEO on the SaaSpocolypse, AI integration and impact on the economy
item.description: The ASX200 closed slightly higher after a late-session rebound, with mining giants BHP and Rio Tinto hitting fresh records while Coles fell after a landmark ACCC court ruling over misleading discount pricing. Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Betashares investment strategist Hugh Lam about the day’s market action and the forces driving investor sentiment. Plus, Xero CEO Sukhinder Singh Cassidy responds to concerns artificial intelligence could disrupt the software-as-a-service sector, outlining how the cloud accounting company plans to navigate the next phase of the AI revolution after reporting a sharp fall in profit.

item.title: Budget reply: From tax reform to migrant caps and welfare cuts, here are six takeaways
item.description: Capping Australia’s net overseas migration and tying it to housing supply are central pillars of the Coalition’s plan.

item.title: 'Pretty strange': Opposition's vision on housing and migration may not add up, experts say
item.description: The Opposition's pitch centres around cuts to migration and measures to boost housing supply. So how do they stack up?

item.title: Branko made a hard choice to sacrifice his citizenship. These Aussies hope to get theirs back
item.description: Thanks to a quirk of Dutch citizenship, thousands of Australians have been denied passports.

item.title: Opposition unveil tax and immigration overhaul | Morning News Bulletin 15 May 2026
item.description: Angus Taylor uses budget reply to unveil tax, immigration and welfare overhaul; Donald Trump says Chinese President offering to help broker peace with Iran; and in sailing, Australia prepares for Americas Cup return.

item.title: Top UK minister resigns as pressure mounts on Keir Starmer
item.description: The UK prime minister has faced calls from within his own party to resign following disastrous local election results for Labour.

item.title: Delta Goodrem takes Australia through to the Eurovision final
item.description: Australia progresses through the semi-finals at the 70th Eurovision Song Contest in Vienna, Austria.

item.title: Donald Trump hails Xi Jinping as a 'great leader' but tensions remain over Taiwan
item.description: The US leader is undertaking a state visit to China for the first time in nearly a decade.

item.title: One Nation copy cat? Opposition accused of borrowing Hanson's policies in budget reply
item.description: Coalition leader Angus Taylor has delivered his first budget reply, vowing to crack down on migration levels and prohibit non-citizens from accessing welfare payments. Critics say the coalition is blaming migrants for Australia's economic troubles, as Pauline Hanson's anti-immigrant One Nation accuses the opposition of copying their policies.

item.title: Trump signals Chinese President "would love to help" in conflict with Iran
item.description: US President Donald Trump says China has offered to help calm conflict between the US and Iran, after high-stakes talks with President Xi Jinping in Beijing that focused heavily on the war in the Middle East, the Strait of Hormuz and growing fears of global economic fallout. The visit also exposed the delicate balancing act facing both powers, with Taiwan, trade and military tensions still threatening to destabilise the relationship between Washington and Beijing.

item.title: Angus Taylor’s budget reply speech criticised for demonising migrants | Midday News Bulletin 15 May 2026
item.description: Angus Taylor’s budget reply speech criticised for demonising migrants; A bus crash in Queensland claims the life of one woman, with foreign nationals among the injured; and in golf, Australian Min Woo Lee among early leaders at the PGA Championship.

item.title: Skilled migration 'complements, not replaces' local workers, agricultural bodies say
item.description: An inquiry into skilled migration will hear from agricultural groups about the vital role migrant workers play in the sector.

item.title: Six passengers in quarantine in Australia after evacuation from hantavirus cruise ship
item.description: Six passengers from a cruise ship at the centre of a deadly hantavirus outbreak have returned to Australia, after the government secured a flight for them. They will now spend three weeks in quarantine which is just short of the World Health Organization's recommended 42-day period.

item.title: Sam Kerr to leave Chelsea after six years and trophy-laden career with London club
item.description: The Matildas captain is expected to make a move to the US National Women's Soccer League team Denver Summit.

item.title: 'Become a citizen': Taylor questioned over welfare payment cuts for permanent migrants
item.description: The Opposition has faced questions about what constitutes an Australian and who will be cut off from welfare payments under his new proposal.

item.title: The Coalition's proposed migration changes slammed | SBS News in Easy English 15 May 2026
item.description: A daily 5 minute news wrap for English learners

item.title: Six passengers from hantavirus-hit cruise ship land in Perth
item.description: The six passengers and the crew that helped repatriate them will now be transferred to a quarantine facility in Perth.

item.title: 'No place in Australia': Burke adds 'neo-Nazis' to proscribed hate group list
item.description: The home affairs minister has used powers established in the wake of the Bondi attack to prohibit a second hate group.

item.title: Hantavirus cruise passengers arrive in Perth | Evening News Bulletin 15 May 2026
item.description: Hantavirus cruise passengers arrive in Australia; Neo-Nazis declared a prohibited hate group in Australia; Aussie Jensen Plowright just off the podium in the Giro d’Italia.

item.title: Could Australia win Eurovision? The odds are looking Goodrem
item.description: Delta Goodrem has progressed to the grand final of the Eurovision Song Contest, where she is one of the favourites to win.

item.title: The 'Thucydides trap' and Taiwan: What the US' visit to China means for Australia
item.description: Xi Jinping has demonstrated his resolve in front of a diminished Donald Trump.

item.title: How AI is rewriting resumes and the ASX ends tough week lower
item.description: On this episode of the SBS On the Money podcast, Stephanie Youssef speaks with Michael Page Senior Managing Director David George about the growing use of artificial intelligence in recruitment, with more job seekers and employers turning to AI to write resumes, job ads and interview questions. Plus, Ricardo Gonçalves is joined by Tribeca Investment Partners Portfolio Manager Todd Warren to unpack a volatile week on the Australian sharemarket, as profit taking in the mining sector helped drag the ASX 1.3% lower for the week.

item.title: Israel and Lebanon agree to extend ceasefire by 45 days | Morning News Bulletin 16 May 2026
item.description: The US says Israel and Lebanon agree to a 45-day ceasefire extension; Hantavirus quarantine patients spend their first night in Australia; and in sport, Tassie Devils VFLW side to play first match.

item.title: The $3.1 trillion tax secret hiding in plain sight
item.description: Some Australians have benefited for decades from a secretive arrangement that allows them to hide assets and reduce tax.

item.title: The $3.1 trillion tax secret hiding in plain sight
item.description: Some Australians have benefited for decades from a secretive arrangement that allows them to hide assets and reduce tax.

item.title: A tax shake-up, wage fight heats up — and a job hunting twist
item.description: The key money stories this week, broken down simply — and what they mean for you.

item.title: INTERVIEW: Assistant Minister for Multicultural Affairs Julian Hill, responds to Opposition's budget reply
item.description: Opposition leader Angus Taylor used his budget reply speech last night to outline the Coalition immigration policies, confirming that includes removing welfare payments and access to the NDIS for permanent residents. He says says the move would save billions of dollars. But Assistant Minister for Citizenship, Customs and Multicultural Affairs, Julian Hill is describing the policy as a "dog whistle to One Nation". SBS Political Correspondent Rayane Tamer spoke to Julian Hill who rejected the Coalition's proposal that excessive migration was contributing to housing barriers.

item.title: INTERVIEW: Angus Taylor questioned by Chief Political Correspondent Anna Henderson
item.description: Opposition leader Angus Taylor says a Coalition government would remove 75,000 visa overstayers from Australia. In his budget reply, he also announced that non-citizens will be blocked from accessing 17 welfare programs. One Nation says Angus Taylor has copied its migration policy. SBS Chief Political Correspondent Anna Henderson asked Mr Taylor whether he's moving further to the right to try to court One Nation voters.

item.title: As Xi sets a new power dynamic, Trump leaves China with little to brag about
item.description: Throughout a largely cordial summit, there were signals that the balance of power between the two leaders might be shifting.

item.title: In the Eurovision press room, Delta Goodrem passed the vibe check
item.description: Finland remains a finals favourite, but Delta has serious momentum. Could it be enough to carry her to a win?

item.title: Hopes for 'lasting peace': Israel and Lebanon extend ceasefire by 45-days
item.description: A US official has described the talks between the countries as "highly productive".

item.title: How Australian housing became a generational budget battleground
item.description: Housing taxation changes have upset some investors. If the government can win the support of young people, that may not matter.

item.title: Next-generation Indigenous women are leading the way on employment growth
item.description: A new study has shown that employment rates for Indigenous men and women are now nearly equal.

item.title: Australia has a growing $56 billion problem - is this a solution?
item.description: As more women face mental health challenges, experts are pushing for more single-gender psychiatric wards.

item.title: Donald Trump warns Taiwan against declaring independence | Midday News Bulletin 16 May 2026
item.description: Donald Trump warns Taiwan against declaring independence; Final preparations ahead of the Eurovision grand final; and in sport, Auckland FC book a spot in the first A-League grand final.

item.title: Eurovision at the Rooty Hill RSL Club?
item.description: Australia is among 10 countries - including Israel and favourite Finland - who have places in this weekend's Eurovision grand final. Chief International Correspondent Ben Lewis spoke with SBS Eurovision commentators Courtney Act and Danny Estrin about Delta Goodrem's chances in the grand final and if Australia could host the contest at the Rooty Hill RSL Club.

item.title: Government to reassess fuel excise extension as reserves swell, July cutoff nears
item.description: Australia remains at level two of a fuel plan that asks users only buy what they need and take voluntary steps to use less.

item.title: Man dead after shark attack on Western Australia's Rottnest Island
item.description: It's the first reported shark attack since January, when a 12-year-old boy died after an attack in Sydney Harbour.

item.title: 'It's as if failure isn't an option': The hidden sting when your home flops at auction
item.description: Auction clearance rates are slipping — and for sellers, a failed auction can leave more than just disappointment.

item.title: Albanese says the government will defend laws prohibiting hate groups | Evening News Bulletin 16 May 2026
item.description: Anthony Albanese says the federal government will defend laws prohibiting hate groups; a man dies after a shark attack in Western Australia; and in AFL, Carlton's interim coach he's not ready for a full-time coaching role.

item.title: INTERVIEW: Greens leader Larissa Waters responds to the Federal Budget
item.description: Greens leader Larissa Waters says the federal government should be setting out to build more public and affordable housing similar to the way previous governments used to build large-scale public housing estates. Senator Waters says housing affordability demands bold reform but says the federal government changes to property taxes in the Budget amounts to tinkering around the edges. SBS Chief Political Correspondent Anna Henderson asked her whether the Greens will back the changes to negative gearing and capital gains.

item.title: Israel’s Eurovision voting campaign is in the spotlight — again. Here’s why
item.description: Israel's public broadcaster was formally warned by Eurovision last week

item.title: Gaza mosques announce Hamas military leader's death
item.description: Witnesses said mosques ⁠announced ‌Izz al-Din al-Haddad's "martyrdom" after Israel's military said ‌airstrikes had ‌targeted ⁠him.

item.title: Hamas military chief Izz al-Din al-Haddad killed in Israeli air strike| Morning News Bulletin 17 May 2026
item.description: Hamas military chief Izz al-Din al-Haddad killed in an Israeli air strike; A fatal shark attack in Western Australia; and in football, Melbourne City win their fifth A-League Women’s championship title.

item.title: These Greek islands are booked, busy, and built up. Now locals are calling for change
item.description: For Australians, Santorini and Mykonos are popular holiday destinations. But locals say their home is being 'destroyed'.

item.title: Prepper used to be a dirty word. But these 'ordinary' people think it's just sensible now
item.description: One lives in the bush for $50 a week. Another grows vegetables in South Australia. Meet Australia's doomsday preppers.

item.title: Antisemitism, Zionism and Israel: Royal commission hearings take a political turn
item.description: Differentiating antisemitism and criticism of Israel was one focal point of the commission's second week of hearings.

item.title: Michelin Guide comes to Australia
item.description: South Australia is to feature in the globally recognised Michelin Guide - the first time any Australian destination will be covered in the guide's history.

item.title: Tanya was struggling to cope with daily tasks. When she found out why, it was life-changing
item.description: One in four women in Australia faces mental health challenges. However, some are at risk when seeking help in mixed-gender hospital wards. An Australian psychiatrist is working to change that.

item.title: The Aussies who could be caught in the middle of the housing tax shake-up
item.description: Who are the Australians who could be caught in the middle of housing tax reforms in the federal budget? The government says changes to negative gearing and capital gains tax are designed to improve housing affordability and level the playing field for younger generations. But some experts warn the reforms could also create unintended consequences for Australians trying to build wealth and break into the property market.

item.title: Driving of car into pedestrians in Italian city was a deliberate act, authorities say
item.description: Italy's interior ministry said the driver was undergoing treatment for psychiatric disorders as the search for a motive continues.

item.title: In the wake of the social media ban, Australia's queer youth are seeking connection
item.description: With social media closed to them, some LGBTIQ+ youth are turning to unmoderated spaces online to find community.

item.title: Eurovision 2026: Bulgaria sweeps grand final as Australia's Delta Goodrem places fourth
item.description: The finals weren't without controversy, boos erupting from the audience as Israel's points totals were announced.

item.title: Who is DARA, the Bulgarian singer who won Eurovision 2026?
item.description: DARA's song Bangaranga swept voting at the famed song competition, beating out Israel, Romania and Australia.

item.title: Coalition courts One Nation voters with hardline migration plan | Midday News Bulletin 17 May 2026
item.description: Angus Taylor defends the Coalition's immigration policies amid heavy criticism; A Canadian who sailed on a hantavirus-hit cruise ship tests positive for the disease; and in football, Sam Kerr scores a winning goal for Chelsea in her last game for the club.

item.title: Taylor vows to regain voter 'trust' but says prospect of One Nation in Coalition is 'nonsense'
item.description: As the Liberal Party loses voters to One Nation, Angus Taylor has played down the idea of expanding the Coalition to include it.

item.title: A major tax deadline is coming next year — could it spark a rush to sell?
item.description: The changes to CGT will take effect starting in July 2027, giving investors time to decide what to do with their properties.

item.title: WHO declares Ebola outbreak an international health emergency
item.description: The outbreak of the highly contagious disease began in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda.

item.title: Fans shower Australia's Eurovision contestant Delta Goodrem with support | Evening News Bulletin 17 May 2026
item.description: In this bulletin, The Prime Minister announces housing development for former Hobart Defence site; Victoria Police reopen hunt for a Melbourne armed robbery case 30 years ago; and in football, Manchester City wins FA Cup final over Chelsea, but no time to celebrate just yet.

item.title: EV battery supply chains in focus as China halts sulphuric acid exports
item.description: China ceasing exports of sulphuric acid is expected to put pressure on both EV battery and fertiliser supply chains

item.title: Fans celebrate ‘sun goddess' Delta Goodrem’s Eurovision Grand Final performance
item.description: Delta Goodrem 'stoked' with Eurovision result, as Sydney fans celebrate her dazzling Grand Final performance

item.title: Migrant Ronil thinks Pauline Hanson is 'fantastic', Neha disagrees. Exploring the One Nation divide
item.description: Australians from across the political spectrum give their views on One Nation following the party's recent by-election victory.

item.title: Bigger than ever: What makes this year's FIFA World Cup unique
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup will span three countries, feature 104 matches and introduce new measures to curb time-wasting.

item.title: 'Huge step forward': Ozempic endorsed to treat obesity and heart disease in Australian first
item.description: Two-thirds of Australians are at risk of cardiovasular disease. Could GLP-1 medications be the answer?

item.title: 'Huge step forward': Ozempic endorsed to treat obesity and heart disease in Australian first
item.description: Two-thirds of Australians are at risk of cardiovasular disease. Could GLP-1 medications be the answer?

item.title: This charity receives millions from the government. Workers say conditions were 'like a jail'
item.description: Migrant workers contracted under the PALM scheme to labour hire company Madec Australia say they are being treated unfairly.

item.title: Trump warns Iran as ceasefire crumbles | Morning News Bulletin 18 May 2026
item.description: Donald Trump warns Iran it is running out of time to negotiate a peace deal; a shark attack victim hailed as a "one-of-a-kind gentleman" and incredible father; and in football, Celtic takes the Scottish Premiership.

item.title: UAE reports drone strike at nuclear power plant as Trump says 'clock is ticking' for Iran
item.description: A drone strike has targeted the United Arab Emirates' sole nuclear power plant, sparking a fire, officials say.

item.title: Ukrainian drone strikes kill four in Russia in its biggest attack in over a year
item.description: Volodymyr Zelenskyy says Ukraine's increased attacks on Moscow have produced a "change in the balance of actions at the front".
